records.ety: [F. appendant, p. pr. of appendre. See Append, v. t.]

defs: Hanging; annexed; adjunct; concomitant; as, a seal appendant to a paper.
quotes: As they have transmitted the benefit to us, it is but reasonable we should suffer the appendant calamity.

defs: Appended by prescription, that is, a personal usage for a considerable time; -- said of a thing of inheritance belonging to another inheritance which is superior or more worthy; as, an advowson, common, etc. , which may be appendant to a manor, common of fishing to a freehold, a seat in church to a house.
Anything attached to another as incidental or subordinate to it.
A inheritance annexed by prescription to a superior inheritance.
